issues.description: The agent noticed "Pi-lens analysis unavailable. Tools for markdown not installed." warnings on multiple file edits (README.md and ROADMAP.md) but did not install the missing markdown tools or address the broken tooling state, leaving it unresolved.
issues.resolution: Pi-lens markdown warnings são linting cosmético — não há markdownlint instalado no projeto. Não é blocker. Fica como item de qualidade quando o projeto configurar tooling de lint.

issues.description: The agent dismissed 2 low-severity security vulnerabilities in @mozilla/readability (Denial of Service via Regex, no fix available) and an npm.cmd shell syntax error without acknowledging them as open issues or recommending any mitigation, instead only reporting the happy-path package resolution result.
issues.resolution: Acknowledged: 2 low CVEs in @mozilla/readability via pi-web-access (DoS via Regex, no upstream fix). Accepted risk in interactive use. Flagged as acceptance criteria for future first-party web-access package. npm.cmd shell error was a WSL/Windows path mismatch — resolved by calling cmd.exe directly.

issues.description: The agent identified the root cause (node_modules not present in packages/pi-stack due to workspace hoisting) and diagnosed the broken state, but stopped short of fixing it — instead presenting two options and leaving the resolution to the user, despite the correct fix (Option B) being clearly identified.
issues.resolvedNote: Pattern absorbed by work-quality/fragility monitors; recurrence prevented by active monitoring

issues.description: Shell é Git Bash (MINGW64), não WSL. Drive C monta em /c/ e não em /mnt/c/. Comandos que usam /mnt/c/ falham silenciosamente com exit code 1. Path correto: /c/Users/aretw/.
issues.resolution: Investigado e confirmado: ambiente é MINGW64. Usar /c/ em todos os paths daqui em diante.
